data_notes: Rollups were used as authoritative for last-7 and baseline-28 training load totals.
Sleep metrics for 2026-03-06..2026-03-12 are missing in the rollup window (sleep_days_with_data=0), so last-7 sleep/HRV/RHR means are left blank rather than estimated.
Baseline 28d includes sleep_hours_mean (8.7h) but baseline HRV/RHR signals were not provided in the Signal registry, so they are not reported.
Planned weekly distance/elevation are targets (not measured from data) while planned time is constrained to remain below the max ramp requirement.
Readiness status was set from deterministic readiness_and_risk_forecast.json.
Guardrails: enforced ramp rate (max_ramp_pct=10.0%). last7_hours=9.533, allowed_hours=10.486, planned_hours=8.200.

meta.style: Trail + bike (deload-leaning week with 2 quality touches)

days.purpose: Create a clear recovery signal after an elevated 7-day load; set up the next 6 days to be productive without stacking fatigue.
days.target_intensity: No training stress today. If you want to move: 20–30 min easy walk only.
days.title: Rest + reset (absorb the elevated 7d load)
days.workout: Off. Optional: 10–15 min mobility (ankles/hips/T-spine) + light leg flush (foam roll).

days.purpose: Maintain aerobic frequency while keeping overall stress controlled this week.
days.target_intensity: Easy conversational / all-day pace. Smooth cadence; avoid surges.
days.terrain: Mostly flat to rolling; minimize long climbs
days.title: Easy aerobic ride (keep it truly easy)
days.workout: 90 min easy. Optional: include 4 × 15 sec high-cadence spin-ups (NOT sprints), full easy recovery between.

days.purpose: Long-session stimulus without making it a hard day; supports endurance while staying within a safe weekly ramp.
days.target_intensity: Easy endurance. You should finish feeling like you could keep going.
days.terrain: Rolling endurance route; keep climbs aerobic (no hard pushes)
days.title: Long aerobic (steady endurance, low intensity)
days.workout: 150 min easy aerobic ride. Fuel it (carbs early + steady) to keep effort honest and prevent late-ride intensity drift.

days.purpose: Reintroduce run frequency without adding intensity; keep neuromuscular touch via short strides only if you feel good.
days.target_intensity: Easy. If breathing/legs feel heavy, shorten to 35–40 min.
days.terrain: Flat to gently rolling; soft surface if possible
days.title: Easy run (economy + tissue conditioning)
days.workout: 50 min easy run. Optional: 4–6 × 10 sec relaxed strides on flat (full easy jog recoveries).

days.purpose: One focused intensity stimulus to maintain fitness while keeping total weekly stress in check.
days.target_intensity: Hard but controlled; keep the last rep as strong as the first. Stop early if form/effort spikes.
days.title: Quality 1: Bike intervals (controlled, repeatable)
days.workout: Warm-up 15 min easy → 4 × 6 min strong (upper-tempo/threshold feel) with 4 min easy between → cool down easy to 75 min total.

days.purpose: Facilitate recovery between hard days; reduce the risk of stacking intensity.
days.target_intensity: Very easy, nose-breathing level; keep it light.
days.title: Recovery spin (or rest if fatigue shows up)
days.workout: 60 min easy spin. If you’re noticeably cooked, swap to complete rest + mobility.

days.purpose: Trail-relevant muscular endurance and economy without excessive total volume; second and final hard session of the week.
days.target_intensity: Strong uphill effort, but never sprinting; smooth form. Full control on descents.
days.terrain: Moderate hill (trail or road); prioritize good footing
days.title: Quality 2: Short hill reps (running-specific strength)
days.workout: Warm-up 20 min easy → 6 × 3 min uphill at strong effort with easy jog/walk back down recoveries → cool down easy to 70 min total.

readiness.rationale: Deterministic readiness is STEADY with moderate overreach risk, driven by elevated 7d training load. With 0 sleep days recorded in the last 7 days, we can’t validate recovery (sleep/HRV/RHR), so the plan prioritizes a slightly conservative load and spacing hard sessions.

actions: Sleep: prioritize consistency this week (fixed wake time). With no sleep data in the last 7 days, treat sleep as the #1 limiter and keep intensity capped if sleep quality is poor.
Fueling: for sessions ≥75 min, start carbs early and steadily; include protein + carbs within ~60 min after hard/long sessions.
Mobility/prehab (10–15 min, 4–6 days/wk): calves/ankles, hips/glutes, thoracic rotation; add light eccentric calf raises if you tolerate them well.
Hydration: arrive hydrated and include electrolytes on longer rides or warm days to avoid turning endurance into a stress session.

risks.message: Moderate overreach risk with elevated 7d training load: keep this week controlled and avoid adding extra intensity/volume beyond the plan.
Recovery visibility is limited: last 7 days show 0 sleep-days with data, so adjust day-to-day based on perceived fatigue and keep hard sessions repeatable rather than maximal.

snapshot.notes: Last 7d load is elevated and sleep data is missing for the entire last-7d window, so recovery signals can’t be confirmed. Keep this week controlled: maintain frequency, reduce spike-risk, and keep intensity to two focused sessions.
